title: Pre-Start Uniface Servers
text: Pre-Start Uniface Servers It is possible for the Uniface Router to automatically start Uniface Servers when the Uniface Router itself starts. This can be useful when: The server to be started is effectively a service that should always be available and that has no direct client connection. You want to have a server already available for a client the moment the client connects to the server. Pre-starting servers ensures that they are available directly after rebooting the machine. Note: Pre-starting a server is a one-time action. The Uniface Router does not restart them if they unexpectedly exit. Such servers can be re-started by using the Uniface Router Monitor or UROUTMON component. To configure the Uniface Router to automatically start Uniface Servers: In the urouter.asn file, add or edit the [PRE_START] section and define the servers to be started. For more information, see [PRE_START] and Network Connection Strings . For example: [PRE_START] TCP:+13002|user|P@55word|server2 Copy Stop and restart the Uniface Router. For more information, see Stop and Start the Uniface Router . Related Links [PRE_START] Use the [PRE_START] section to specify the Uniface Servers that the Uniface Router should automatically start. Each line specifies a server to be started and provides the same information that a client would provide when requesting a server. Network Connection Strings Network connection strings are used to specify the network location of a Uniface Router, Uniface Server, or other server, such as SMTP or POP server. They can be used in path assignments, command line strings, and parameters to identify a target application, or to describe the current application. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
